data.description:

data.title: 'There Will Be No Bailout For Bitcoin,' VanEck's Matthew Sigel Warns
data.description:
Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) has pulled back 50% from its peak, but volatility has also declined sharply from prior cycles, according to Matthew Sigel, VanEck's head of digital assets research.

50% Price Drop, Lower Volatility

Speaking on CNBC's Market Alert on Friday, Sigel described the current correction as significant but structurally different from past bear markets.

"There is no CEO of Bitcoin, there will be no bailout," Sigel said, noting that regulatory uncertainty, including delays around the Clarity Act, may have contributed to selling pressure.

He added that market jitters intensified following disclosures that members of the Trump family sold part of their stake in World Liberty Financial.

Despite the 50% price decline, Sigel emphasized that realized volatility has also dropped about 50% compared with the 2022 correction, when Bitcoin plunged roughly 80%.

Based on Bitcoin's historical four-year cycle, he argued that the 2022 bear market has already passed and the current period represents a typical down year within a broader cycle.

13 Countries Mining BTC

Addressing whether Bitcoin remains necessary in a world increasingly focused on regulated stablecoins, Sigel said BTC plays a crucial role for individuals in high-inflation economies or those lacking access to U.S. dollars.

He added that some countries with excess energy capacity are allocating a portion of that supply to mine Bitcoin, strengthening monetary sovereignty.

According to Sigel, "13 different countries" are currently mining Bitcoin at the central government level.

Sigel also noted that leverage in Bitcoin markets is relatively low compared with other asset classes, as institutional adoption has expanded across Wall Street.

He said that trend has supported VanEck's growth in the digital asset space.

While prices remain under pressure, Sigel's thesis suggests the structural backdrop for Bitcoin may be stronger, and less volatile, than in previous downturns.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: AI Pivot Buzz Sends Agencia Comercial Spirits Stock Higher
data.description:
Agencia Comercial Spirits Ltd. (NASDAQ:AGCC) stock rose Friday as the company explores new opportunities in AI computing infrastructure.

This positive movement comes amid a mixed performance in the broader market, with major indices showing slight declines.

AGCC’s Leap Into AI Infrastructure

Agencia has signed two nonbinding letters of intent (LOIs) as it considers diversifying beyond its core Taiwan-based whisky import and distribution business.

The LOI requires a $3.5 million deposit within five business days, refundable if no definitive agreement is reached and there is no material breach. Although Ricloud is listed in Nvidia’s partner directory, AGCC noted that Nvidia has not confirmed supply or delivery capacity, and directory inclusion does not constitute an endorsement.

The second LOI concerns a proposed 50,000-square-meter data center site in Indonesia, potentially through a new affiliate. Booking payments total $180,000 plus 3 billion Indonesian Rupiah and 11% VAT, and are described as nonrefundable if cancelled.

Both LOIs are preliminary and subject to due diligence, financing, regulatory approvals, and definitive agreements.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 18.9% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 16.7% above its 50-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 91.90%, and they are currently closer to their 52-week highs than their lows.

The RSI is at 60.72, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum. The combination of a neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating potential for further gains if the stock maintains its current trajectory.

  • Key Resistance: $14.50
  • Key Support: $10.00

The recent exploration into AI computing infrastructure and data centers represents a significant shift in strategy, potentially opening new revenue streams and diversifying its business model. This move could enhance the company’s market position and provide growth opportunities beyond its traditional whisky operations.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Agencia Comercial Spirits, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 1.28 — The stock is performing poorly relative to its peers.

The Verdict: Agencia Comercial Spirits’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Value score indicates significant overvaluation, the company’s strategic pivot into AI infrastructure could offer new growth avenues.

AGCC Price Action: Agencia Comercial Spirits shares were up 9.39% at $12.70 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Moolec Science (MLEC) Stock Explodes 90% After GLA Update
data.description:
• Moolec Science stock is showing exceptional strength. What’s behind MLEC gains?

GLASO Results Drive Investor Focus On MLEC Stock

The program produced oil with about 45% gamma-linolenic acid after standard U.S. crushing, which the company says is among the highest levels reported at commercial scale.

Moolec cultivated about 1,100 acres of engineered safflower in its 2025 campaign, with average yields near 2,200 pounds per acre, a 57% year-over-year increase from roughly 1,400 pounds per acre in 2024.

Management said the results validate the platform's ability to run through existing U.S. agricultural and industrial infrastructure, a key step toward repeatable commercial production.

Market Reaction, Outlook For Moolec Science

In early trading, MLEC shares traded around $11.12, up roughly 118% on the day, as investors reacted to the update. The company plans to pursue an execution-led growth strategy focused on scaling proven platforms, maintaining cost discipline and targeting recurring revenue in the nutrition and dietary-supplements market.

MLEC Price Action: Moolec Science shares were up 78.63% at $9.11 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: US Stocks Edge Lower; Advance Auto Parts Posts Upbeat Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stocks traded lower this morning, with the Dow Jones index falling around 0.1% on Friday.

Following the market opening Friday, the Dow traded down 0.13% to 49,387.55 while the NASDAQ fell 0.23% to 22,545.67. The S&P 500 also fell, dropping, 0.04% to 6,830.79.

Check This Out: How To Earn $500 A Month From Goldman Sachs Stock Ahead Of Q4 Earnings

Leading and Lagging Sectors

Utilities shares gained by 1.5% on Friday.

In trading on Friday, communication services stocks fell by 0.6%.

Top Headline

Advance Auto Parts, Inc. (NYSE:AAP) reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results.

Advance Auto Parts reported quarterly earnings of 86 cents per share which beat the analyst consensus estimate of 42 cents per share. The company reported quarterly sales of $1.973 billion which beat the analyst consensus estimate of $1.952 billion.

Equities Trading UP
           

Equities Trading DOWN

Commodities

In commodity news, oil traded down 0.3% to $62.67 while gold traded up 1.4% at $5,016.60.

Silver traded up 2.1% to $77.285 on Friday, while copper fell 0.8% to $5.7420.

Euro zone

European shares were lower today. The eurozone's STOXX 600 fell 0.5%, while Spain's IBEX 35 Index fell 1.6%. London's FTSE 100 slipped 0.1%, Germany's DAX fell 0.1% and France's CAC 40 dipped 0.6% during the session.

Asia Pacific Markets

Asian markets closed lower on Friday, with Japan's Nikkei falling 1.21%, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index falling 1.72%, China's Shanghai Composite dipping 1.26% and India's BSE Sensex declining 1.25%.

Economics

The annual inflation rate eased to 2.4% in January, recording its lowest level since May, compared to 2.7% in the previous month and down from market estimates of 2.5%.

Photo via Shutterstock
